mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over 3 consecutive months signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk, triggering equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS declines for 3+ months, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above its 12-month trend while unemployment remains below 4.0%, signaling robust consumer demand and economic momentum that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 697,557 million dollars and UNRATE at 3.8% as of 2023-08-01
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of instances where both RSAFS accelerates and UNRATE < 4.0%, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously, indicating supply-side weakness coupled with labor market softening that reduces earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp MoM
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.49 and UNRATE at 3.8% as of 2023-08-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously, or joint probability of negative returns is not significantly higher than baseline (chi-square p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,400 thousand units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.0% annualized, signaling demand-side weakness across both residential investment and aggregate output that depresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1400 thousand units AND GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,452 thousand units and GDPC1 at 20,386.467 billion as of 2023-07-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where both HOUST < 1400 and GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC between composite demand signal and forward returns < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL decelerates below 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains above 5.0%, indicating the Fed's restrictive policy is successfully controlling inflation and setting up a future easing cycle that will support equity valuations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 306.269 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2023-08-01
regime_hint: late-stage monetary tightening with inflation moderating
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where inflation decelerates below 3.0% despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or forward returns do not show significant positive skew relative to baseline (Wilcoxon p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains below 4.0% for 6+ consecutive months while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows steadily, signaling a persistently tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0% for 6+ consecutive months AND PAYEMS growth > 0 MoM
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% and PAYEMS at 156,419 thousand as of 2023-08-01
regime_hint: tight labor market with full employment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 4.0% for 6+ months and PAYEMS grows, or correlation between labor market tightness composite and 5-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows above its 12-month trend while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable, indicating genuine supply-side productivity gains without inflationary pressure that support profit margins and produce positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.49 and CPIAUCSL at 306.269 as of 2023-08-01
regime_hint: any regime with positive supply dynamics
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO grows above trend, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and forward returns < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
